content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research to break the local optimization trap of single-perspective iterative self-feedback. This creates a hybrid self-improvement approach that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or labeled data while fixing its common flaw of converging to suboptimal outputs due to biased initial self-feedback.
